title: Cat Vomiting Undigested Food Daily: Causes and Veterinary Triage
meta_desc: Is your cat vomiting undigested food every day? Learn about urgency levels, potential causes, and how to help your vet diagnose the issue.
content_html:
What Is Happening When a Cat Vomits Undigested Food?

When your cat brings up food that looks exactly as it did in the bowl, it is often a sign of regurgitation or a rapid rejection of stomach contents. This daily occurrence suggests that the food is not moving through the digestive tract properly. Common causes range from eating too quickly—often called scarf and barf—to more complex issues like food sensitivities, esophageal disorders, or chronic conditions like inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Urgency Level: Medium

Daily vomiting of undigested food is classified as a Medium Urgency situation. While it may not be an immediate life-threatening crisis if your cat is still behaving normally, a daily frequency indicates a chronic problem that requires a veterinary exam within the next 24 to 48 hours. If the vomiting is accompanied by weight loss, lethargy, or a refusal to drink water, the urgency level increases significantly.

Why a Photo Helps During Triage

It may feel strange to take a picture of your cat's vomit, but it is one of the most helpful tools for a veterinary professional. A photo allows the vet to see the exact texture, color, and volume of the material. This helps them distinguish between true vomiting and regurgitation, and identifies whether the food was partially digested or if there is any evidence of blood, bile, or foreign objects, leading to a faster and more accurate diagnosis.

faqs.question: Could my cat be eating too fast?
faqs.answer: Yes, many cats vomit undigested food because they swallow too much air or consume kibble too quickly. Try using a slow-feeder bowl or a puzzle feeder to see if the frequency decreases.

faqs.question: Is it normal for a cat to vomit every day?
faqs.answer: No, it is never considered normal for a cat to vomit daily. Even if they seem fine otherwise, chronic vomiting is a sign of an underlying health issue that needs professional evaluation.

faqs.question: What should I do if my cat stops eating entirely?
faqs.answer: If your cat stops eating for more than 24 hours in addition to daily vomiting, this becomes a high-priority situation. Contact your veterinarian immediately as cats can develop serious liver issues very quickly when they stop eating.
